commit.message:
fix(pb): clear actor reschedule ts on alloc (#4338)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions (#4381)

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions

* fix(swift): mark test suite sendable

* fix(swift): avoid isolated deinit

* chore(ci): disable swift tests

* chore(ci): restore full frontend checks

* fix(ci): restore full repo checks

* fix(workflow-engine): align biome format

* feat(frontend): polish onboarding UI (#4376)

* feat(frontend): make onboarding local-first

Add two new onboarding steps before cloud deployment:
1. "Set up your project" - clone example and run locally (manual) or
   MCP/skills setup with agent instructions (agent flow)
2. "Explore Rivet" - RivetKit Inspector info and links to docs/examples

Move SkillsSetup and TemplateSetup from BackendSetup to the new local
step. BackendSetup now focuses solely on cloud deployment configuration.

Also fix www.rivet.dev -> rivet.dev and rivet-gg -> rivet-dev URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rivetkit): add "start key" and "end key" to validateKvKey label type

The KV range operations added in #4372 pass "start key" and "end key"
as keyLabel arguments but the type only accepted "key" | "prefix key".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: rivet
repository.description: Rivet Actors are the primitive for stateful workloads. Built for AI agents, collaborative apps, and durable execution.

commit.message:
fix(api): properly dedup name list responses (#4337)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

repository.name: rivet
repository.description: Rivet Actors are the primitive for stateful workloads. Built for AI agents, collaborative apps, and durable execution.

commit.message:
fix(ups): formalize subjects (#4334)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move SkillsSetup and TemplateSetup from BackendSetup to the new local

commit.message:
Fix fuzz test crash caused by Zig 0.15.2 self-hosted backend bug (#8)

## Summary

Fixes the fuzz test CI failure caused by a known Zig 0.15.2 bug
([ziglang/zig#23423](https://github.com/ziglang/zig/issues/23423)).

## Root Cause

The Zig self-hosted x86 backend (used by default in Debug builds)
doesn't emit the sanitizer-coverage instrumentation sections
(`__sancov_pcs1` / `__sancov_cntrs`) that the build runner's fuzz
infrastructure requires. When the fuzzer tries to process coverage data,
it finds an empty program counter array and crashes with:

```
thread 2934 panic: start index 1 is larger than end index 0
std/Build/Fuzz.zig:429:17: in addEntryPoint
    for (pcs[1..], 1..) |elem_addr, i| {
```

This affects all Zig 0.15.x versions and is also reported as
[#25798](https://github.com/ziglang/zig/issues/25798) and
[#25470](https://github.com/ziglang/zig/issues/25470).

## Fix

1. **`build.zig`**: Added a dedicated `fuzz` build step that:
   - Forces `use_llvm = true` to ensure proper coverage instrumentation
- Uses `ReleaseSafe` optimization (recommended for fuzzing throughput)
   - Filters to only fuzz test functions (those named `fuzz:*`)

2. **`.github/workflows/fuzz.yml`**: Changed from `zig build test
--fuzz` to `zig build fuzz --fuzz`

## Verification

- Reproduced the crash locally with the original configuration
- Verified the fix: fuzz tests run without crashing (exit code 124 from
timeout = success)
- Verified existing unit tests (`zig build test`) still pass

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jveski <7576912+jveski@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: bump rivetkit protocol version (#4329)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

Co-authored-by: tfritzy <23142109+tfritzy@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(udb): add txn tagging (#4328)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

* feat(frontend): make onboarding local-first

Add two new onboarding steps before cloud deployment:
1. "Set up your project" - clone example and run locally (manual) or
   MCP/skills setup with agent instructions (agent flow)
2. "Explore Rivet" - RivetKit Inspector info and links to docs/examples

Move SkillsSetup and TemplateSetup from BackendSetup to the new local
step. BackendSetup now focuses solely on cloud deployment configuration.

Also fix www.rivet.dev -> rivet.dev and rivet-gg -> rivet-dev URLs.

as keyLabel arguments but the type only accepted "key" | "prefix key".

repository.name: rivet
repository.description: Rivet Actors are the primitive for stateful workloads. Built for AI agents, collaborative apps, and durable execution.

commit.message:
fix(api): make actor destroy wait for ack (#4327)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: rivet
repository.description: Rivet Actors are the primitive for stateful workloads. Built for AI agents, collaborative apps, and durable execution.

commit.message:
chore: flatten runtime config (#4326)

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions (#4381)

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions

* fix(swift): mark test suite sendable

* fix(swift): avoid isolated deinit

* chore(ci): disable swift tests

* chore(ci): restore full frontend checks

* fix(ci): restore full repo checks

* fix(workflow-engine): align biome format

* feat(frontend): polish onboarding UI (#4376)

* feat(frontend): make onboarding local-first

Add two new onboarding steps before cloud deployment:
1. "Set up your project" - clone example and run locally (manual) or
   MCP/skills setup with agent instructions (agent flow)
2. "Explore Rivet" - RivetKit Inspector info and links to docs/examples

Move SkillsSetup and TemplateSetup from BackendSetup to the new local
step. BackendSetup now focuses solely on cloud deployment configuration.

Also fix www.rivet.dev -> rivet.dev and rivet-gg -> rivet-dev URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rivetkit): add "start key" and "end key" to validateKvKey label type

The KV range operations added in #4372 pass "start key" and "end key"
as keyLabel arguments but the type only accepted "key" | "prefix key".

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

commit.message:
chore: deploy patch (#4324)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

commit.message:
feat: implement sonde-gateway Phase 2A foundation (#26)

* feat: implement sonde-gateway Phase 2A foundation

Scaffold the sonde-gateway crate and build all 6 foundation modules
with 17 validation tests passing.

Modules:
- crypto.rs: RustCryptoHmac (constant-time verify) + RustCryptoSha256
  implementing sonde-protocol traits
- transport.rs: async Transport trait + MockTransport (inbound queue,
  outbound capture, PeerAddress)
- storage.rs: async Storage trait + InMemoryStorage (tokio RwLock)
- registry.rs: NodeRecord with key_hint lookup, battery/ABI tracking,
  program hash confirmation
- session.rs: Session/SessionManager/SessionState with create/replace,
  sequence verification, state transitions, expiry reaping
- program.rs: ProgramRecord/ProgramLibrary with CBOR image storage,
  SHA-256 hash computation, size enforcement, chunk serving

Tests (17 passing):
- T-0100: no unsolicited transmission
- T-0700/0702/0703: node registry, battery tracking, ABI version
- T-0604/0605/0606/0607/T-1004: session replay protection, replacement,
  wrong seq, no session, timeout
- T-0400/0401/0405/0406/0407: program ingestion, rejection, hash
  identity, hash covers maps, size enforcement
- Crypto: HMAC round-trip, wrong-key rejection, SHA-256 determinism

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR #26 review -- 4 issues

- session.rs: verify_and_advance_seq checks session expiry inline
  (removes expired session and returns SessionError::Expired) instead
  of relying on background reap_expired() call
- session.rs: add SessionError::Expired variant
- program.rs: reject empty images with ProgramError::InvalidImage
- Cargo.toml: remove unused rand dependency
- T-1004 test: verify timeout via verify_and_advance_seq (not manual
  reap), matching the validation spec procedure
- T-0401 test: verify empty image is rejected

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: clarify Transport::recv doc for real vs mock behavior

Real transports await until a frame arrives; test mocks may return
TransportError::NoMoreFrames immediately when the queue is empty.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CI: remove dotnet-quality preview, drop explicit FSharp.Core ref

- Remove dotnet-quality: 'preview' from ci.yml and nuget-publish.yml
  (.NET 10 is now GA; the preview flag caused SDK version conflicts on
  the macOS runner which has 10.0.103 pre-installed, conflicting with
  the RC being installed by setup-dotnet)
- Remove DisableImplicitFSharpCoreReference and explicit FSharp.Core
  Version='9.0.*' from SageTUI.Library.fsproj — that version range
  silently skipped net9.0/net10.0 target generation during restore on
  the GA SDK. Let the SDK inject the correct FSharp.Core per TFM
  automatically (the default behavior).

commit.message:
Automate PyPI publish on push to master

Replaces manual release-triggered workflow with auto-detect:
- On push to master, checks if sun_hit_detector/ or pyproject.toml changed
- If yes: bumps patch version, commits [skip ci], tags, creates release, publishes to PyPI
- If only custom_components/ or other files changed: skips (no unnecessary publish)
- GITHUB_TOKEN pushes don't re-trigger workflows (no infinite loops)

HACS sees new versions via GitHub releases, PyPI gets the core library.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore(docker): Refactor sandbox state management and improve Docker integration (#1068)

* Refactor sandbox state management and improve Docker integration

- Removed FileSandboxStateStore and SandboxStateStore classes for a cleaner architecture.
- Enhanced LocalContainerBackend to handle port allocation retries and introduced environment variable support for sandbox host configuration.
- Updated Paths class to include host_base_dir for Docker volume mounts and ensured proper permissions for sandbox directories.
- Modified ExtensionsConfig to improve error handling when loading configuration files and adjusted environment variable resolution.
- Updated sandbox configuration to include a replicas option for managing concurrent sandbox containers.
- Improved logging and context management in SandboxMiddleware for better sandbox lifecycle handling.
- Enhanced network port allocation logic to bind to 0.0.0.0 for compatibility with Docker.
- Updated Docker Compose files to ensure proper volume management and environment variable configuration.
- Created scripts to ensure necessary configuration files are present before starting services.
- Cleaned up unused MCP server configurations in extensions_config.example.json.

* Address Copilot review suggestions from PR #1068 (#9)

commit.message:
Fix all button failures: consolidate 6 sheet modifiers into one

DashboardView had 6 separate .sheet(isPresented:) modifiers on the same
view — SwiftUI only honors the last one and silently breaks all touch
events on the others. Replace all 6 with a single .sheet(item:) driven
by a DashboardSheet enum. Store booleans (showEndInteractionSheet,
showStartShift) are synced via .onChange. This is the root cause of
every 'buttons not working' report.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Virtual input system review (#2936)

* fix: virtual input system review — 6 fixes for keyboard/mouse overlay gaps

Fixes #2930: Auto-hide virtual keyboard when USB/Bluetooth keyboard connects
- Observe GCKeyboardDidConnect/GCKeyboardDidDisconnect notifications
- Track whether keyboard was auto-hidden so it restores on disconnect

Fixes #2931: Apply effectiveKeyboardOverlayConfig variant/opacity
- Map VirtualKeyboardVariant -> VirtualKeyboardLayout via toLayout()
- Use skin config or user preference for initial layout
- Apply configured opacity to hosting view

Fixes #2932: ensureProperZOrder() now includes virtual input overlays
- Call bringVirtualInputOverlaysToFront() during rotation/layout changes

Fixes #2933: Add virtual mouse show/hide toggle to pause menu
- New coreSupportsVirtualMouse, isVirtualMouseVisible properties
- New showVirtualMouse/hideVirtualMouse/toggleVirtualMouse methods
- VIRTUAL MOUSE button in RetroMenuView CORE tab

Fixes #2934: preferredKeyboardVariant setting now used
- Read on keyboard show, write when user switches layout
- Resolution: skin config > user preference > system default > .full

Fixes #2935: Re-enable onscreenJoypadWithKeyboard logic
- Uncomment adjustJoystick() check for keyboard + setting

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

* fix: address Copilot review — keyboard layout priority and joypad visibility

- resolvedKeyboardLayout() now checks: (1) explicit skin JSON config,
  (2) user's preferredKeyboardVariant, (3) DeltaSkinDefaults, (4) .full.
  Previously the user preference was unreachable for systems with a
  default config (DOS, C64, MSX, etc.).

- adjustJoystick() now sets joyPad.isHidden = false when the
  keyboard-connected condition is not met, so the joypad restores
  after a hardware keyboard is disconnected.

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

* Update PVUI/Sources/PVUIBase/SwiftUI/DeltaSkins/Models/DeltaSkinTypes.swift

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply keyboard hide/show to joyPad2 in adjustJoystick() (#2938)

* Initial plan

* fix: hide/show joyPad2 with joyPad on keyboard connect in adjustJoystick()

Co-authored-by: JoeMatt <399864+JoeMatt@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: JoeMatt <399864+JoeMatt@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address remaining Copilot review comments

- Hide/show joyPad2 alongside joyPad in adjustJoystick() so both
  joystick overlays respect onscreenJoypadWithKeyboard consistently.
- Add @MainActor to the virtual mouse extension so UIKit view
  hierarchy mutations are guaranteed to run on the main thread.
- Change hwKeyboardObservers type from [Any]? to [NSObjectProtocol]?
  for type-safety matching NotificationCenter.addObserver return type.

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

* fix: prevent virtual keyboard flash when hardware keyboard is connected

Start hardware keyboard observation before showing the virtual keyboard
overlay in setupVirtualInputOverlaysIfNeeded(). If a hardware keyboard
is already connected (GCKeyboard.coalesced != nil), skip the auto-show
entirely to avoid a visible show-then-hide flicker on launch.

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

* fix: prevent virtual keyboard flash when hardware keyboard already connected

Check GCKeyboard.coalesced before showing the virtual keyboard on
launch. Previously the overlay was shown then immediately hidden
when startObservingHardwareKeyboard() detected the existing
hardware keyboard, causing a visible flicker. Now the hardware
keyboard check runs first and skips the show entirely, while still
setting keyboardHiddenByHardware so disconnecting later will
auto-restore the overlay.

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

---------

feat: Foundry agents lifecycle - CI/CD best practices demo

Complete educational demo for CI/CD of Microsoft Foundry AI agents.

Infrastructure (Bicep IaC):
- Foundry resource (AIServices + allowProjectManagement)
- Foundry project as child resource
- Model deployments (gpt-4o-mini)
- Key Vault for secrets management
- Subscription-scope orchestrator with dual-pipeline isolation

CI/CD Pipelines:
- GitHub Actions (OIDC auth, deploy agent, run evals)
- Azure DevOps (service connections, same workflow)
- Both pipelines target separate resource groups for isolation

Agent Lifecycle Scripts:
- deploy_agent.py: SDK-driven agent creation from config
- test_agent.py: Interactive agent testing with conversation support
- run_evaluation.py: Automated evaluation with scoring

Documentation:
- MkDocs site with architecture, mental model, quickstart
- Per-pipeline guides (GitHub Actions + Azure DevOps)
- Config schema reference (SCHEMA.md)
- FAQ and troubleshooting

Key insight: Foundry agents have no export/import — CI/CD is
SDK-driven (delete + recreate from code). This demo teaches that.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

player count, showing e.g. '1/8' when there was 1 human + 3 CPU.
Now reports total players including CPU opponents.

Closes #121

Multiple pushes to uat during promotion (manual merge + PR merge)
each triggered deploy-uat.yml, causing double Discord notifications.

Add concurrency group with cancel-in-progress so only the latest
push completes the full deploy + notify pipeline.

Closes #119

commit.message:
Refactor/dotfiles optimization (#7)

* refactor: optimize dotfiles structure and fix bugs

- Fix code quality issues: duplicate code, syntax errors, security issues
- Standardize code style: use #!/usr/bin/env bash, add set -euo pipefail
- Improve error handling: modular functions, logging, better validation
- Create lib/common.sh shared library for reusable functions
- Fix permissions in update_github_hosts function

* feat(bootstrap): add smart installation modes

Add new command-line options for flexible installation:
- -c, --config-only: Only run setup, skip tool installation
- -u, --update: Force tool installation/update
- -h, --help: Show help message

Smart behavior:
- First run (no ~/.dotfiles_initialized): setup + tool installation
- Subsequent runs: only setup by default
- --update flag forces tool installation on any run

Fix: Use global variables instead of command substitution
to allow proper exit on --help flag.

This allows users to quickly update dotfiles config without
reinstalling all tools, while still supporting full updates
when needed.

* Update minimal

commit.message:
test: add fuzz tests for validation and crypto packages (#108)

Add fuzz tests for validation package:
- FuzzEmail, FuzzUUID, FuzzPhone, FuzzURL: regex-based validators
- FuzzMinLength, FuzzMaxLength: length constraint validators

Add fuzz tests for crypto package:
- FuzzHMACSHA256: HMAC signing + round-trip verification
- FuzzEqual: constant-time comparison correctness

All fuzz tests seeded with representative inputs and verified
with 2-3 second fuzzing runs (no crashes or panics).

commit.message:
Add ESP32 port in esp32/ subfolder

- WiFi.h / HTTPClient.h instead of ESP8266 equivalents
- IRAM_ATTR instead of ICACHE_RAM_ATTR for ISRs
- portDISABLE/ENABLE_INTERRUPTS for critical sections
- ESP32 VSPI default pins (MOSI=23, SCLK=18, CS=5, DC=2, RST=4)
- Encoder on GPIO32/33/25, KEY0 on GPIO26
- Updated README with ESP32 wiring table + setup steps
- .gitignore now also excludes esp32/config.h
